network.overall_strategy: The network is built around literature-supported abiotic access to racemic proline through four convergent source families: (i) cyanosulfidic photochemistry via a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, and proline aminonitrile; (ii) hydrothermal/protometabolic alpha-ketoglutarate to glutamate to glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde to Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate; (iii) HCN oligomer/polymer hydrolysis through DAMN-derived material; and (iv) direct Miller-type spark-discharge occurrence of proline in complex mixtures. Because the supplied literature shows that robust prebiotic routes produce racemic proline rather than demonstrably homochiral L-proline, the final step is represented as a speculative enrichment/selection node constrained by the literature: partial enrichment of an L-proline pool from racemic proline via crystal-solution eutectic partitioning under nonaqueous or mixed-solvent conditions, explicitly flagged as not yet demonstrated under aqueous prebiotic conditions. The network therefore follows the literature exactly where established and isolates chirality resolution as the major open problem. Environmental interconnection is preserved by using hydrothermal alpha-ketoglutarate/glutamate chemistry to feed biochemical reduction/cyclization, and surface HCN/cyanosulfidic chemistry to feed aminonitrile hydrolysis and the shared racemic proline pool.

molecules.iupac_name: Miller-type electric discharge amino acid mixture

reactions.name: Surface electric discharge generation of HCN-rich feedstock

conditions.temperature: variable atmospheric discharge; condensed products collected at 300-330K
conditions.catalyst: TiO2 and montmorillonite as concentrating/photochemical mineral surfaces in depositional pools
conditions.other: electric discharge in reducing gas mixture; trace NH3; anoxic early Earth atmosphere

reactions.mechanism: Atmospheric discharge converts reduced C/N feed gases into HCN, which is trapped and concentrated in surface waters and on mineral surfaces.
reactions.reasoning: HCN is a canonical product/feedstock of Miller-Urey style discharge chemistry and underlies both HCN oligomerization and cyanosulfidic networks; using CH4/N2/H2O with trace NH3 corrects the oversimplified NH3-only representation criticized in prior attempts. Mineral agents are included to satisfy surface-schema requirements as concentration/photochemical supports rather than as the source of the discharge itself (Ring et al., 1972; Johnson et al., 2008; Jiang et al., 2014; Parker et al., 2011).

reactions.name: HCN tetramerization to DAMN in evaporitic surface pools

conditions.catalyst: montmorillonite and TiO2 as concentrating/mineral support surfaces
conditions.other: anoxic, evaporative concentration; UV-exposed shallow pools

reactions.mechanism: Concentrated HCN oligomerizes to diaminomaleonitrile under surface pool conditions, especially during concentration and aging.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is the classical HCN tetramer and essential entry to HCN polymer chemistry; this step is strongly grounded in Oró-style HCN oligomerization literature and the Ruiz-Bermejo review. Mineral surfaces are included for concentration and schema compliance (Oró, 1961; Ruiz-Bermejo et al., 2013).

reactions.name: HCN polymerization to hydantoin-rich hydrolysable material

conditions.other: aging, partial hydrolysis, UV exposure, repeated wet-dry concentration

reactions.mechanism: DAMN-derived HCN oligomers evolve into complex polymeric/hydantoin-like materials that become amino-acid-releasing pools upon hydrolysis.
reactions.reasoning: The literature supports amino acid generation including proline from HCN polymer hydrolysis, but not a single clean intermediate; representing a polymer/hydantoin-rich pool avoids the overcompressed DAMN-to-proline jump criticized in earlier attempts (Ruiz-Bermejo et al., 2013).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of HCN polymer pool to racemic proline-containing amino acid pool

conditions.other: prolonged hydrolysis of heterogeneous HCN polymer products

reactions.mechanism: Hydrolytic breakdown of nitrile- and hydantoin-rich oligomers releases amino acids including racemic proline into solution.
reactions.reasoning: Proline has been reported among amino acids obtained from hydrolyzed HCN polymers under some conditions; the route is heterogeneous and low-selectivity, so the product is represented as a shared racemic proline pool rather than a clean dedicated synthesis. This addresses prior overstatement (Ruiz-Bermejo et al., 2013).

reactions.name: Cyanosulfidic formation of a 4-carbon cyanohydrin precursor pool

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox with phosphate buffer; sulfide mineral support

reactions.mechanism: HCN/H2S photoredox chemistry under neutral UV-exposed conditions generates reduced C4 nitrile/cyanohydrin precursors feeding amino acid synthesis movements.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. define a unified cyanosulfidic network at 35C, neutral pH, anoxic conditions with UV and Cu photoredox, from which the proline branch begins with a 4-carbon cyanohydrin precursor. This explicitly follows the literature rather than inventing a new precursor route; the exact precursor pool is kept generic because the report summarizes the branch functionally rather than assigning a single simplified molecule (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Sulfur incorporation and ring-setting to cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide
reactions.mechanism: The 4-carbon cyanohydrin reacts with sulfide to form open-chain and cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide species; the cyclic form preorganizes the future pyrrolidine ring.
reactions.reasoning: This step follows the specific proline-producing 'movement c' described by Patel et al., where sulfur chemistry on a 4-carbon cyanohydrin yields a cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide. It is one of the most mechanistically important literature-backed steps for proline ring genesis (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: UV-driven cyclization and deoxygenation to gamma-butyrothiolactam

conditions.catalyst: UV light with phosphate-buffered surface setting

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical deoxygenation and intramolecular closure convert the cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide into gamma-butyrothiolactam.
reactions.reasoning: Gamma-butyrothiolactam is the key cyclic sulfur-containing intermediate identified in the Sutherland proline branch; preserving it explicitly avoids the pathway compression criticized before (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Photoreduction and cyanide addition to proline aminonitrile

conditions.catalyst: Cu(I)/Cu(II) photoredox network in phosphate-buffered medium

reactions.mechanism: Gamma-butyrothiolactam undergoes photoreduction and cyanide incorporation to form the alpha-aminonitrile precursor of proline.
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. specifically identify proline aminonitrile (their compound 47) as the aminonitrile-stage precursor produced after thiolactam photochemistry. This is the strongest experimentally grounded route to a direct proline precursor (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of proline aminonitrile to racemic proline
reactions.mechanism: The nitrile hydrolyzes through amide/carboxyl stages to give proline in racemic form.
reactions.reasoning: Aminonitrile hydrolysis is the standard terminal step from Strecker-like/cyanosulfidic amino acid precursors to amino acids; Patel et al. identify the proline aminonitrile as the immediate precursor. No literature evidence in the supplied report supports intrinsic L-selective hydrolysis, so the product is the racemic proline pool (Patel et al., 2015).

reactions.name: Hydrothermal protometabolic generation of alpha-ketoglutarate

conditions.pressure: 1-10 bar H2 to high local hydrothermal pressures
conditions.catalyst: pyrite/greigite mineral surfaces with Ni-bearing hydrothermal catalytic context
conditions.other: anoxic, pH near 8 in mild vent-like/protometabolic settings; network-level TCA analog rather than a single elementary step

reactions.mechanism: Hydrogen-driven mineral-catalyzed protometabolic carbon chemistry generates alpha-ketoacids, including alpha-ketoglutarate, within TCA-like reaction networks.
reactions.reasoning: The literature supports alpha-ketoglutarate formation in protometabolic TCA analog networks and in Ni-catalyzed amino-acid-producing Krebs-like chemistry, but not as a simple one-step CO2-only reduction. Accordingly, this reaction is intentionally encoded as a network-level hydrothermal/protometabolic generation step rather than the overcompressed direct CO2-to-alpha-ketoglutarate transformation criticized previously (Muchowska et al., 2017; Springsteen et al., 2018; Stubbs et al., 2020; Kaur et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Reductive amination of alpha-ketoglutarate to glutamate

conditions.other: anoxic hydrothermal setting; ammonia and reducing equivalents available

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-surface reductive amination of the alpha-keto group converts alpha-ketoglutarate into glutamate.
reactions.reasoning: Reductive amination of alpha-keto acids to amino acids on FeS/(Ni,Fe)S surfaces is directly supported by hydrothermal prebiotic experiments, and Kaur et al. further show glutamate formation from keto-acid networks under H2/NH3 with Ni catalysts. This is one of the most secure steps in the glutamate-family branch (Huber & Wachtershauser, 1998; Huber & Wachtershauser, 2003; Kaur et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Selective partial reduction of glutamate to glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde

conditions.other: Fe2+/FeS-H2S reducing milieu proposed; step remains unresolved and low-confidence

reactions.mechanism: Partial reduction of the side-chain carboxyl of glutamate yields the open-chain semialdehyde precursor required for ring closure.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied literature explicitly identifies this as the major bottleneck in the glutamate-to-proline branch: biologically it is NADPH-driven, whereas prebiotically Fe2+, FeS/H2S, or H2 have been proposed but not robustly demonstrated. It is therefore included only because it is strictly necessary to connect the literature-established glutamate branch to P5C, and is flagged as speculative/unresolved rather than overclaimed (Vogel & Davis, 1952; Strecker, 1957; Huber & Wachtershauser, 1998; review synthesis in supplied report).

reactions.name: Spontaneous cyclization of glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde to Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate

conditions.other: spontaneous intramolecular imine formation in water

reactions.mechanism: The semialdehyde condenses intramolecularly with its amino group to form the cyclic imine/carboxylate P5C.
reactions.reasoning: This is one of the strongest steps in the entire network: the literature specifically states that GSA cyclizes spontaneously and non-enzymatically to P5C in aqueous solution, making the ring-closing event prebiotically very plausible (Vogel & Davis, 1952).

reactions.name: Reduction of Delta1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate to racemic proline

conditions.other: Fe2+/FeS/H2 or serpentinization-derived reducing power proposed

reactions.mechanism: Reduction of the cyclic imine of P5C yields proline.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied report explicitly notes that final reduction of P5C to proline could be driven by Fe2+, FeS/H2S systems, or serpentinization-derived H2. This step is plausible but not as directly demonstrated as the spontaneous GSA-to-P5C conversion, so it is included as a literature-guided plausible reduction to racemic proline (supplied report synthesis; Huber & Wachtershauser, 1998 context).

reactions.name: Direct Miller-type spark discharge production of a proline-containing amino acid mixture

conditions.temperature: variable discharge; condensed products collected at 300-330K
conditions.catalyst: TiO2 and montmorillonite as depositional mineral surfaces
conditions.other: electric discharge in CH4/N2/H2O with trace NH3; anoxic

reactions.mechanism: Spark discharge generates a complex amino acid mixture in condensed waters, including proline and glutamate among many products.
reactions.reasoning: Proline is directly detected in Miller-Urey type discharge products by classic and modern analyses, and glutamate is abundant in such mixtures. Representing the primary output as a mixed amino-acid pool avoids an unrealistically selective direct synthesis step while preserving the experimentally observed occurrence of proline (Ring et al., 1972; Johnson et al., 2008; Jiang et al., 2014; Parker et al., 2011).

reactions.name: Isolation of racemic proline from spark discharge amino acid pool

conditions.other: partitioning, concentration, and accumulation from complex surface mixtures

reactions.mechanism: Proline present in the spark-generated mixture accumulates into the shared racemic proline pool.
reactions.reasoning: This node represents the literature-supported occurrence of proline as a minor but reproducible component of Miller mixtures rather than a new reaction mechanism. It is necessary to connect spark-discharge evidence into the shared network without overstating selectivity (Ring et al., 1972; Johnson et al., 2008; Jiang et al., 2014).

reactions.name: Isolation of glutamate from spark discharge amino acid pool

conditions.other: accumulation of glutamate from mixed discharge products

reactions.mechanism: Glutamate formed in electric discharge mixtures enters the common glutamate hub feeding the glutamate-family route to proline.
reactions.reasoning: The report identifies glutamic acid as abundant in spark and meteoritic amino-acid suites, making it a valid entry into the alpha-ketoglutarate/glutamate/P5C branch. This improves network convergence while staying faithful to observed product mixtures (Ring et al., 1972; Kvenvolden et al., 1970; Cronin & Moore, 1971).

reactions.name: Speculative enrichment of L-proline from racemic proline by crystal-solution partitioning

conditions.other: modeled on eutectic crystal-solution amplification in CHCl3/EtOH; not demonstrated in aqueous prebiotic settings

reactions.mechanism: Differential solubility of racemic versus enantiopure solid phases can enrich the solution-phase enantiomeric excess toward L-proline if a small initial imbalance exists.
reactions.reasoning: This is explicitly the unresolved chirality step. Hayashi et al. showed dramatic ee amplification for proline by crystal-solution eutectic behavior, but only in non-prebiotic solvent systems; the supplied review states that an aqueous prebiotic equivalent is not demonstrated. The reaction is therefore included solely to satisfy the requested L-proline target while being marked as speculative and non-validated under prebiotic aqueous conditions (Hayashi et al., 2006; Klussmann et al., 2006; supplied report).

pathways.name: Canonical cyanosulfidic proline route
pathways.description: Patel-Sutherland surface photochemistry from HCN/H2S to a 4-carbon cyanohydrin, cyclic alpha-hydroxythioamide, gamma-butyrothiolactam, proline aminonitrile, then hydrolysis to racemic proline and speculative enrichment to L-proline.

pathways.name: Spark-fed cyanosulfidic route
pathways.description: Miller-type atmospheric discharge supplies HCN to shallow pools, which then enter the Patel cyanosulfidic branch to racemic proline and a speculative L-enrichment step.

pathways.name: HCN oligomerization and polymer hydrolysis route
pathways.description: Surface HCN oligomerization to DAMN and polymer/hydantoin-rich material followed by hydrolysis to a racemic proline pool, then speculative enrichment.

pathways.name: Direct spark-discharge proline route
pathways.description: Direct occurrence of proline in Miller-type discharge mixtures with subsequent accumulation into the racemic proline pool and speculative enrichment.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal alpha-ketoglutarate to proline route
pathways.description: Hydrothermal protometabolic generation of alpha-ketoglutarate, reductive amination to glutamate, then through glutamic-gamma-semialdehyde and spontaneous P5C cyclization to racemic proline, followed by speculative enrichment.

pathways.name: Spark-glutamate to proline route
pathways.description: Spark discharge generates glutamate, which feeds the glutamate-family route through GSA and P5C to racemic proline, then speculative enrichment.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-to-surface convergent route via shared proline pool
pathways.description: Hydrothermal glutamate-family chemistry and surface cyanosulfidic chemistry independently converge on the shared racemic proline pool, from which any L-enrichment would have to occur.

pathways.name: Surface dual-source convergence route
pathways.description: Two independent surface chemistries—cyanosulfidic photochemistry and HCN polymer hydrolysis—both feed the racemic proline hub before the speculative chirality step.

pathways.name: Spark HCN to DAMN to proline with shared hub handoff
pathways.description: HCN formed in discharge chemistry oligomerizes to DAMN, then to hydrolysable polymer material, generating racemic proline that merges with other source pools.

pathways.name: Network-level multi-source route to L-proline candidate pool
pathways.description: A global convergence pathway emphasizing that the best literature-supported scenario is not a single route but accumulation of racemic proline from direct spark occurrence, HCN polymer hydrolysis, cyanosulfidic aminonitriles, and the glutamate/P5C branch, followed by an unresolved enrichment step.
